title: The Incomplete Record
description: A patient transfers from another system; the EHR is sparse, so the system must rely heavily on anamnesis data while clearly flagging the gaps.

alert.notes: Newly transferred patient; no established baseline on file.

gold_brief.alert_summary: Blood pressure 165/94.9 mmHg on a loaner cuff, above the 140/90 threshold. Baseline unknown — this is a newly transferred patient. Classified Routine pending more data.
gold_brief.patient_snapshot: 61-year-old woman establishing care after an out-of-state move. Reports long-standing hypertension, but outside records have not yet arrived; no medications, labs, or confirmed history are in the system.

contextual_analysis.statement: The patient reports she ran out of her antihypertensives (a 'water pill and a small white tablet') about three weeks ago during the move and has not refilled them.
The elevated reading is consistent with recent cessation of unspecified antihypertensive therapy.

risk_assessment.statement: Uncontrolled hypertension with a family history of stroke warrants timely treatment, though she is currently asymptomatic.

recommended_actions.action: Establish care: obtain the outside records and reconcile her prior medications.
Restart antihypertensive therapy per clinical judgment, since she is currently untreated.
Confirm home BP monitoring and schedule near-term follow-up.

uncertainties_and_gaps: The EHR is sparse: no medication list, no labs, and no confirmed problem list — outside records were requested but not received.
Exact prior medications are unknown (the patient could not name them).
No reliable baseline blood pressure is available for comparison.
